title: Google introduces Gemma 4 12B for laptop-scale multimodal AI
summary: Google released Gemma 4 12B, a mid-sized open model with text, vision, and native audio input, designed to run on laptops with around 16GB of VRAM or unified memory under an Apache 2.0 license.
friendlySummary: Google released Gemma 4 12B, an open model that can work with text, images, and audio on capable laptops.
whyItMatters: Local multimodal models reduce dependence on cloud inference for private, low-latency assistants. Developers can prototype audio- and vision-aware workflows on everyday hardware instead of waiting for large hosted systems.
friendlyWhyItMatters: More AI work can happen privately and quickly on local hardware, instead of always relying on cloud services.
